title: OXIPRO's Computational Platforms: Enhancing Enzyme Discovery with Horus
author: Guallar, Victor
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
What if enzyme discovery didn’t have to mean months in the lab and mountains of data? 
 Meet  Horus  — OXIPRO’s powerful new platform that’s changing how we approach enzyme engineering. 
 Built by the Barcelona Supercomputing Center, Horus combines high-performance computing, AI-driven design, and an intuitive graphical interface to make enzyme modelling accessible to everyone from molecular biologists to industrial innovators. 
 It’s not just another tool. It’s a modular, visual,  collaboration-first  system that simulates enzyme behaviour before you ever pick up a pipette. 
 👁️ Named after the Egyptian god of vision, Horus brings clarity to complexity — from de novo protein design to docking simulations. 
 Explore how Horus is helping democratise access to computational biology and fast-track innovation in sustainable biotech.
